title: How a Cambridge home cook built a 1,000-member recipe club around local ingredients
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Cambridge Recipe Club, founded by Andrew Little, focuses on local ingredients and aims to build community through shared recipes.
The club has over 1,000 members receiving weekly newsletters with recipes proofed by Little's partner, accessible to Greater Boston residents.
Little encourages community participation by featuring member-contributed recipes and collaborating with local chefs for content.

title: Campbell to continue suit against Live Nation, as internal messages show employee mocked customers
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts AG Andrea Campbell will proceed with an antitrust lawsuit against Live Nation, despite a DOJ settlement.
Live Nation employee's derogatory Slack messages about customers are to be used as evidence in the antitrust case.
Live Nation controls over 265 venues in North America, with Ticketmaster handling 80% of major ticket sales, per Campbell's office.

title: Healey, Campbell launch portal for reporting ICE misconduct
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey and Attorney General Andrea Campbell launched a portal for reporting ICE misconduct in Massachusetts.
The portal allows users to submit photos, videos, and reports of alleged ICE misconduct and other federal agents.
The initiative aims to identify misconduct patterns and support possible legal actions or policy recommendations against federal agents.

title: Jaylen Brown criticizes NBA officials for allowing foul-baiting, rewarding flopping
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticizes NBA officiating for rewarding flopping and foul-baiting after Celtics' loss to Oklahoma City.
Brown expressed frustration, suggesting he might need to flop more to get favorable calls in crucial plays.
Celtics have struggled, losing three of their last five games; they face the Wizards at home on Saturday.

title: Oscars 2026 predictions: Full list of who will win each category
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "One Battle After Another" is favored to win Best Picture at the 2026 Oscars, but "Sinners" could upset due to ranked choice voting.
Michael B. Jordan, nominated for "Sinners," is predicted to win Best Actor over Timothee Chalamet and other contenders.
Paul Thomas Anderson is expected to win Best Director for "One Battle After Another," with stiff competition from Ryan Coogler for "Sinners."

title: Why new Patriots safety Kevin Byard predicted New England’s breakthrough season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kevin Byard joined the Patriots signing a one-year, $9 million contract, reuniting with coach Mike Vrabel.
Byard led the NFL with seven interceptions last season and has never missed a game due to injury.
Byard aims to boost the Patriots' secondary, alongside Christian Gonzalez and Marcus Jones.

title: ‘Our small way of saying thank you’: A mother-son duo is running the Boston Marathon for MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lisa and Benjamin Levine from Needham are running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital (MGH).
The Levines express gratitude to MGH for saving family members' lives and supporting Benjamin's food allergy management.
The Boston Marathon run honors the efforts of MGH providers, staff, educators, and researchers who improve lives daily.

title: $25M winning lottery ticket sold at Turners Falls convenience store
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Trustee Renat Lumpau accepted a one-time payment of $16,250,000 for a $25 million lottery ticket.
The winning ticket was purchased at Cumberland Farms on Montague City Road in Turners Falls, Massachusetts.
Cumberland Farms will receive a $50,000 bonus for selling the winning $25 million ticket.

title: After prior uproar over removal, new mayor to restore Italian flag road lines to Newton
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Newton Mayor Marc Laredo will restore Italian flag road lines on Adams Street in Nonantum.
The red, white, and green markings were previously removed for safety compliance under former Mayor Ruthanne Fuller.
New parking lane markings allow for the restoration of the symbolic lines by modifying roadway width.

title: Student charged in Danvers murder allegedly told police he ‘wanted to kill someone for a long time’
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, 18, charged with murder and home invasion in Janet Swallow's death in Danvers, pled not guilty.
DeMayo confessed to planning the murder, claiming he wanted to kill someone for a long time, according to police.
Judge ordered DeMayo to undergo psychiatric evaluation before the April 1 hearing; held without bail.
